gaps: No test case covers the scenario of a newly paired device appearing in the device list after pairing.
No test case verifies that turning on an already-displayed device updates its status to 'connected'.
No test case covers device list refresh behavior after pairing (without app restart).
No test case covers device connection status updates in the device list.

generated_testcases.title: Verify newly paired device appears in device list without app restart
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the device list screen.
Note the current list of paired devices.
Pair a new device using the system Bluetooth settings or in-app pairing flow.
Return to the app's device list screen.
Observe the device list without restarting the app.
Verify the newly paired device is displayed in the list.
generated_testcases.expected: The newly paired device appears in the device list immediately after pairing, without requiring an app restart.

generated_testcases.title: Verify turning on an already-displayed device updates its status to 'connected'
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the device list screen.
Ensure a previously paired device is displayed in the list but is currently turned off or disconnected.
Turn on the device (e.g., power it on or make it discoverable).
Observe the status indicator for that device in the app's device list.
Verify the status updates to 'connected'.
generated_testcases.expected: The device status changes from 'disconnected' or 'off' to 'connected' in the device list after the device is turned on.

generated_testcases.title: Verify device list refreshes after pairing a new device (without app restart)
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the device list screen.
Record the current number of devices shown.
Pair a new device via the app's pairing flow or system settings.
Wait for the pairing process to complete.
Observe the device list for any refresh indicator (e.g., loading spinner, list update animation).
Verify the device list now includes the newly paired device and the count has increased by 1.
generated_testcases.expected: The device list automatically refreshes after pairing, showing the newly added device without requiring the user to restart the app.

generated_testcases.title: Verify device connection status updates in real-time in the device list
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the device list screen.
Identify a device that is currently disconnected (status shows 'disconnected' or 'off').
Trigger a connection event (e.g., turn on the device, bring it within range, or tap 'Connect').
Monitor the device list for status changes without manual refresh.
Verify the device status updates to 'connected' within a reasonable time (e.g., 5-10 seconds).
Trigger a disconnection event (e.g., turn off the device or move out of range).
Verify the device status updates back to 'disconnected'.
generated_testcases.expected: The device list reflects real-time connection status changes, updating from 'disconnected' to 'connected' when the device connects, and back to 'disconnected' when it disconnects.

generated_testcases.title: Verify device list consistency after multiple pairing and connection status changes
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the device list screen.
Pair two new devices sequentially without restarting the app.
Verify both newly paired devices appear in the list.
Turn on one of the already-displayed devices and verify its status changes to 'connected'.
Turn off that device and verify its status changes back to 'disconnected'.
Turn on the second device and verify its status changes to 'connected'.
Verify that all devices remain visible and their statuses are correctly reflected throughout the sequence.
generated_testcases.expected: The device list correctly displays all paired devices and accurately reflects their connection statuses after multiple pairing and status change operations, without requiring app restarts.
